claim: Multi-agent observability is essential once you scale beyond 1-2 agents; without visibility into what agents are doing, you can't debug, control, or steer the system
actionable: Implement event streaming from all Claude Code agents to centralized observability dashboard before scaling to 3+ parallel agents
source_title: I Can SEE EVERYTHING: Claude Code Hooks for Multi Agent Observability

claim: Claude Code hooks enable one-way event streaming: hooks send HTTP POST to server → stored in SQLite → broadcast via WebSocket to client UI
actionable: Set up Claude Code hooks in .claude/hooks/ directory; configure each hook to POST events to a Bun server; use WebSocket to stream events to frontend
source_title: I Can SEE EVERYTHING: Claude Code Hooks for Multi Agent Observability

claim: Use Haiku (small fast model) to summarize Claude Code events before sending to server; costs <$0.02 per thousand events for instant summaries at a glance
actionable: Invoke Haiku in hook pre-tool-use and post-tool-use events to generate natural language summaries; display summaries in observability UI instead of raw JSON
source_title: I Can SEE EVERYTHING: Claude Code Hooks for Multi Agent Observability

claim: Multiple Claude Code agents (5-10+) can run in parallel; hooks fire regardless of execution context (local, VPS, multiple devices); all events aggregate in one system
actionable: Deploy Claude Code hooks that POST to a centralized server; scale to multiple agents/machines without modifying observability infrastructure
source_title: I Can SEE EVERYTHING: Claude Code Hooks for Multi Agent Observability

claim: Session IDs + color-hashing allow visual tracking of which agent is doing what work across different codebases; 'live activity pulse' shows event rate per agent
actionable: Use Claude Code session IDs to differentiate agents; hash session ID+app name for consistent colors in observability dashboard
source_title: I Can SEE EVERYTHING: Claude Code Hooks for Multi Agent Observability

claim: Claude Code stop event contains full chat transcript; copy entire log for later analysis or replay; enables post-hoc debugging without live monitoring
actionable: On agent stop, capture full transcript from stop event; search transcripts for specific tool usage patterns to understand agent behavior offline
source_title: I Can SEE EVERYTHING: Claude Code Hooks for Multi Agent Observability

claim: Agentic layer pattern: wrap codebase with generative AI scaffolding (hooks, agents, orchestration); this layer sits around your actual application code
actionable: Structure projects with an agentic layer (in .claude/ or similar) that orchestrates work on the actual app layer; keep concerns separated
source_title: I Can SEE EVERYTHING: Claude Code Hooks for Multi Agent Observability
